text:
Once upon a time in Sunnyvale lived an otter girl. Her name was Olive. She loved exploring.

One spring day olive went hunting. The hunt was easy. She just floated around her favorite pond with her shiny fish-catching net. That's where she saw them. Hundreds of fish swimming peacefully.

But not just any fish. These were flying fish. Olive knew she couldn’t catch them normally.

She tried harder. She jumped higher. She even had an idea.

She tied feathers onto her tail — lots of feathers!

That afternoon she leaped higher than she ever thought POSSIBLE.

Into a big storm clouds.

The wind took her!

It's a long leap!

She soared between clouds!

Then she plunged down —

A great white tuna! Olive grabbed onto its nose!

It flicked angrily!

But Olive wasn't going anywhere.

She flew!

And then —

An eagle!

It swooped!

That bird couldn't bear olive's feathers.

But Olive was strong!

She rode the eagle
